title: How to make several ssh keys in Ubuntu for manage different repositories in GitHub
subtitle: In this tutorial, I will explain how to make a ssh key for different repositories.
excerpt: In this tutorial, I will explain how to make a ssh key for different repositories.

content.text: How to make several ssh keys in Ubuntu for manage different repositories in GitHub

content.html: In this tutorial, I will explain how to make a ssh key for different repositories.

content.text: Make ssh key in Ubuntu system

content.code: ssh-keygen -t rsa -b 4096 -C "you@example.com"ssh-keygenGenerating public/private rsa key pair.Enter file in which to save the key (/home/name/.ssh/id_rsa): id_rsa_personalEnter passphrase (empty for no passphrase): Enter same passphrase again: Your identification has been saved in id_rsa_personalYour public key has been saved in id_rsa_personal.pubThe key fingerprint is:

content.alt: How to make several ssh keys in Ubuntu for manage different repositories in GitHub

content.code: cat configHost github.com    User user1    Hostname github.com    IdentityFile ~/.ssh/id_rsa    IdentitiesOnly yes                Host github2    User user2    Hostname github.com    IdentityFile ~/.ssh/id_rsa_personal    IdentitiesOnly yes

content.text: Add email and username in folder
